input.headline: How Guinness World Records began 7 decades ago

raw.headline: How Guinness World Records began 7 decades ago

winner: A man once tried to break the Guinness World Record for the longest time spent holding a single beer can. He made it to 4 minutes before realizing he was missing the most important part—taking the cap off.

candidates.text: I tried to break the world record for most beers consumed without puking, but they disqualified me for *overwhelming* performance.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat funny with a play on 'overwhelming performance,' but it lacks specificity to the headline and feels slightly generic. It doesn't directly tie to the history of Guinness World Records."}

candidates.text: I tried breaking a world record last week—jumping from a table after eating too much Guinness. The referee said I was disqualified for "excessive beer-induced athleticism.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat funny with the wordplay on 'excessive beer-induced athleticism,' but it lacks specificity to the actual history of Guinness World Records and feels a bit generic in its approach."}

candidates.text: The Guinness family were so bored of record-breaking that they started a new sport—*not* breaking records, just *standing* on them and pretending not to notice when someone else tried.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative and specific, but the humor falls flat due to a lack of clear connection to the actual history of Guinness World Records. The premise is surprising but doesn't align well with the headline's factual context, reducing its overall impact."}

candidates.text: A man once tried to break the Guinness World Record for the longest time spent holding a single beer can. He made it to 4 minutes before realizing he was missing the most important part—taking the cap off.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the humor feels a bit forced and lacks strong punch. The setup about breaking a record for holding a beer can is clever, but the punchline about forgetting to take the cap off doesn't land as effectively due to its predictability and weak connection to the premise."}

candidates.text: When I set a world record, I just eat three hot dogs and wait for someone to challenge me. The paperwork is surprisingly light.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 4.5, "reason": "Lacks specificity to the topic and offers a generic, modern take without historical context or surprise."}
